meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to master user onboarding and overcome project paralysis. A beginner-level checklist for students using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'no project idea' trap stems from lack of focus, not lack of creativity. By building a robust user onboarding flow, you solve a universal SaaS problem. This process forces you to learn authentication, state management, and UX design, turning a simple requirement into a high-value portfolio centerpiece.
content.intro:
The blinking cursor on a blank IDE is the graveyard of a thousand dreams. You stare at the screen, paralyzed by the 'what should I build?' dilemma, while your motivation slowly evaporates. It is not that you lack the skill; you lack the constraints that force innovation.

True engineering mastery isn't found in building the next social network, but in solving the mundane friction that makes users leave apps. By focusing on user onboarding, you transform a generic idea into a polished, professional experience. This is how you stop being a student who codes and start being a developer who builds solutions.

steps.title: Architect the User Journey
steps.description: Define the path from sign-up to 'Aha!' moment. Map out exactly what data you need to capture to provide value immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a user onboarding journey map for a SaaS task manager. Identify the minimal data points required to show immediate value to a new user.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a user flow diagram for an onboarding process using Mermaid syntax'

steps.title: Scaffold the Authentication Layer
steps.description: Implement secure auth. Don't waste time on custom backends; use industry-standard providers to focus on the UI/UX experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a boilerplate for a Next.js 14 app with Clerk authentication and a protected dashboard route.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a Next.js project with Clerk and define a protected /dashboard route'

steps.title: Build the Interactive Walkthrough
steps.description: Create a guided tour that highlights key features. This is where your app moves from a static page to an interactive environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Develop a React component using framer-motion to create a 3-step feature tour popup for a new user dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a React modal component for feature onboarding with framer-motion'

steps.title: Implement Progress Tracking
steps.description: Gamify the onboarding process with a progress bar. Users are more likely to finish onboarding if they see how far they have left.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom hook for managing multi-step onboarding state and a progress bar component that updates based on completion percentage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement a custom useOnboarding hook and a Progress component'

steps.title: Final Deployment and Polish
steps.description: Deploy your project to a production environment. A live URL is your most valuable asset for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Vercel deployment configuration and an automated test suite to ensure the onboarding flow works on mobile and desktop.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Deploy the current directory to Vercel and run a build check'

pro_tips: Always focus on the 'Time to First Value' (TTFV) metric; if your onboarding takes more than 60 seconds, you have already lost the user.
Use mock data early to visualize the UI state before integrating complex backend logic.
Implement 'Empty States'—they are the perfect place to teach users how to use your app when they first sign up.
Add a 'Skip' button. Forcing users through a long onboarding process is the fastest way to trigger churn.
Always log user drop-off points during onboarding to identify which steps are confusing or redundant.
content.next_steps: After completing your onboarding module, add a feedback loop to your app and begin building the core feature set based on the data you collected during the onboarding phase.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-grade component that proves you understand user psychology and system architecture. This is exactly what employers look for in a junior developer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
